chunk_summary: This section outlines centile-based thresholds and time-bound checkpoints for tele-nutrition pathways addressing growth faltering and failure to thrive, emphasizing escalation protocols and measurable severity tiers for effective management.
learning_objectives: Understand centile crossing thresholds for identifying growth faltering in children.
Learn time-bound response checkpoints for tele-nutrition interventions.
Recognize severity tiers and escalation protocols in tele-nutrition pathways.
Apply clinical reasoning using NICE guidelines and India-specific thresholds.
key_facts: Falling across 1, 2, or 3 weight centile spaces raises concern depending on the starting centile band.
Weight below the 2nd centile triggers concern regardless of birthweight centile.
Neonates typically stop losing weight after 3–4 days and regain birth weight by 3 weeks; weight loss >10% requires feeding assessment.

Low risk: stable child, reliable measurements, no threshold crossing; Moderate risk: thresholds met but no danger signs; High risk: persistent weight loss, severe underweight, suspected organic disease.
At 2 weeks, reassess weight trend; lack of improvement triggers in-person evaluation. At 4 weeks, persistent issues despite adherence require diagnostic workup and specialist input.
reference_quote: Concern is raised by falling across 1, 2, or 3 weight centile spaces depending on the child’s starting centile band, or by current weight below the 2nd centile regardless of birthweight centile. (NICE NG75)
document_key_topics: Centile crossing thresholds for growth faltering
Clinical reasoning using NICE and India-specific guidelines
